abstract.para: These functions change the position and shape of geometries using affine transformations.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Apply a 3D affine transformation to a geometry.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Applies a 3D affine transformation to the geometry to do things like translate, rotate, scale in one step.

Version 1: The call ST_Affine(geom, a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, i, xoff, yoff, zoff) represents the transformation matrix / a b c xoff \ | d e f yoff | | g h i zoff | \ 0 0 0 1 / and the vertices are transformed as follows: x' = a*x + b*y + c*z + xoff y' = d*x + e*y + f*z + yoff z' = g*x + h*y + i*z + zoff All of the translate / scale functions below are expressed via such an affine transformation.

Version 2: Applies a 2d affine transformation to the geometry. The call ST_Affine(geom, a, b, d, e, xoff, yoff) represents the transformation matrix / a b 0 xoff \ / a b xoff \ | d e 0 yoff | rsp. | d e yoff | | 0 0 1 0 | \ 0 0 1 / \ 0 0 0 1 / and the vertices are transformed as follows: x' = a*x + b*y + xoff y' = d*x + e*y + yoff z' = z This method is a subcase of the 3D method above.
Enhanced: 2.0.0 support for Polyhedral surfaces, Triangles and TIN was introduced.
Availability: 1.1.2. Name changed from Affine to ST_Affine in 1.2.2

note.para: Prior to 1.3.4, this function crashes if used with geometries that contain CURVES. This is fixed in 1.3.4+

refsection.para: &P_support;
&T_support;
&Z_support;
&curve_support;

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: Rotate a 3D line 180 degrees about the z axis. Note this is long-hand for doing ST_Rotate();
Rotate a 3D line 180 degrees in both the x and z axis.
refsection.programlisting: SELECT ST_Affine(geom, cos(pi()), -sin(pi()), 0, sin(pi()), cos(pi()), -sin(pi()), 0, sin(pi()), cos(pi()), 0, 0, 0) FROM (SELECT 'LINESTRING(1 2 3,1 4 3)'::geometry As geom) As foo;

refnamediv.refpurpose: Rotates a geometry about an origin point.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Rotates geometry rotRadians counter-clockwise about the origin point. The rotation origin can be specified either as a POINT geometry, or as x and y coordinates. If the origin is not specified, the geometry is rotated about POINT(0 0).
Enhanced: 2.0.0 support for Polyhedral surfaces, Triangles and TIN was introduced.
Enhanced: 2.0.0 additional parameters for specifying the origin of rotation were added.
Availability: 1.1.2. Name changed from Rotate to ST_Rotate in 1.2.2
&Z_support;
&curve_support;
&P_support;
&T_support;

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: This example rotates a geometry by 180 degrees.
This example rotates a geometry 30 degrees counter-clockwise around x=50, y=160.
This example rotates a geometry 60 degrees clockwise around its centroid.
refsection.programlisting: SELECT ST_Rotate(geom, -pi()/3, ST_Centroid(geom)) FROM (SELECT 'LINESTRING (50 160,50 50,100 50)'::geometry AS geom) AS foo;

refnamediv.refpurpose: Rotates a geometry about the X axis.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Rotates a geometry geomA - rotRadians about the X axis.

note.para: ST_RotateX(geomA, rotRadians) is short-hand for ST_Affine(geomA, 1, 0, 0, 0, cos(rotRadians), -sin(rotRadians), 0, sin(rotRadians), cos(rotRadians), 0, 0, 0).

refsection.para: The optional pointOrigin parameter rotates around a point origin instead of the coordinate origin. If a 2D geometry is rotated around a 3D point origin, the geometry Z is treated as 0, so the origin Z can affect the output Y coordinate.
Availability: 1.1.2. Name changed from RotateX to ST_RotateX in 1.2.2
Enhanced: 2.0.0 support for Polyhedral surfaces, Triangles and TIN was introduced.
Enhanced: 3.7.0 support for rotating around a point origin was introduced.
&P_support;
&Z_support;
&T_support;

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: This example rotates a line 90 degrees around the X axis.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Rotates a geometry about the Y axis.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Rotates a geometry geomA - rotRadians about the y axis.

note.para: ST_RotateY(geomA, rotRadians) is short-hand for ST_Affine(geomA, cos(rotRadians), 0, sin(rotRadians), 0, 1, 0, -sin(rotRadians), 0, cos(rotRadians), 0, 0, 0).

refsection.para: The optional pointOrigin parameter rotates around a point origin instead of the coordinate origin. If a 2D geometry is rotated around a 3D point origin, the geometry Z is treated as 0, so the origin Z can affect the output X coordinate.
Availability: 1.1.2. Name changed from RotateY to ST_RotateY in 1.2.2
Enhanced: 2.0.0 support for Polyhedral surfaces, Triangles and TIN was introduced.
Enhanced: 3.7.0 support for rotating around a point origin was introduced.
&P_support;
&Z_support;
&T_support;

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: This example rotates a line 90 degrees around the Y axis.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Rotates a geometry about the Z axis.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Rotates a geometry geomA - rotRadians about the Z axis.

note.para: This is a synonym for ST_Rotate
ST_RotateZ(geomA, rotRadians) is short-hand for SELECT ST_Affine(geomA, cos(rotRadians), -sin(rotRadians), 0, sin(rotRadians), cos(rotRadians), 0, 0, 0, 1, 0, 0, 0).

refsection.para: Enhanced: 2.0.0 support for Polyhedral surfaces, Triangles and TIN was introduced.
Availability: 1.1.2. Name changed from RotateZ to ST_RotateZ in 1.2.2

note.para: Prior to 1.3.4, this function crashes if used with geometries that contain CURVES. This is fixed in 1.3.4+

refsection.para: &Z_support;
&curve_support;
&P_support;
&T_support;

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: This example rotates a line 90 degrees around the Z axis.
This example rotates a curved circle around the Z axis.
refsection.programlisting: SELECT ST_RotateZ(geom, pi()/2) FROM (SELECT ST_LineToCurve(ST_Buffer('POINT(234 567)'::geometry, 3)) As geom) As foo;

refnamediv.refpurpose: Rotates a geometry about the X, Y and Z axes.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Rotates a geometry about the X, Y and Z axes, applying the rotations in X, Y, Z order. The optional pointOrigin parameter rotates around a point origin instead of the coordinate origin. If a 2D geometry is rotated around a 3D point origin, the geometry Z is treated as 0, so the origin Z can affect the output X and Y coordinates.
Availability: 3.7.0
&P_support;
&Z_support;
&T_support;

refnamediv.refpurpose: Scales a geometry by given factors.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Scales the geometry to a new size by multiplying the ordinates with the corresponding factor parameters.

The version taking a geometry as the factor parameter allows passing a 2d, 3dm, 3dz or 4d point to set scaling factor for all supported dimensions. Missing dimensions in the factor point are equivalent to no scaling the corresponding dimension.

The three-geometry variant allows a "false origin" for the scaling to be passed in. This allows "scaling in place", for example using the centroid of the geometry as the false origin. Without a false origin, scaling takes place relative to the actual origin, so all coordinates are just multiplied by the scale factor.

note.para: Prior to 1.3.4, this function crashes if used with geometries that contain CURVES. This is fixed in 1.3.4+

refsection.para: Availability: 1.1.0.
Enhanced: 2.0.0 support for Polyhedral surfaces, Triangles and TIN was introduced.
Enhanced: 2.2.0 support for scaling all dimension (factor parameter) was introduced.
Enhanced: 2.5.0 support for scaling relative to a local origin (origin parameter) was introduced.
&P_support;
&Z_support;
&curve_support;
&T_support;
&M_support;

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: This example scales X, Y, and Z values.
This example scales X and Y values.
This example scales X, Y, Z, and M values.
This example scales X and Y values using a false origin.

refnamediv.refpurpose: Translates a geometry by given offsets.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Returns a new geometry whose coordinates are translated delta x,delta y,delta z units. Units are based on the units defined in spatial reference (SRID) for this geometry. M coordinates are preserved.

note.para: Prior to 1.3.4, this function crashes if used with geometries that contain CURVES. This is fixed in 1.3.4+

refsection.para: Availability: 1.2.2
&Z_support;
&curve_support;
&M_support;

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: Move a point 1 degree longitude
Move a linestring 1 degree longitude and 1/2 degree latitude
Move a 3d point
Move points with a measure coordinate
Move a curve and a point

refnamediv.refpurpose: Translates and scales a geometry by given offsets and factors.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Translates the geometry using the deltaX and deltaY args, then scales it using the XFactor, YFactor args, working in 2D only.

note.para: ST_TransScale(geomA, deltaX, deltaY, XFactor, YFactor) is short-hand for ST_Affine(geomA, XFactor, 0, 0, 0, YFactor, 0, 0, 0, 1, deltaX*XFactor, deltaY*YFactor, 0).
Prior to 1.3.4, this function crashes if used with geometries that contain CURVES. This is fixed in 1.3.4+

refsection.para: Availability: 1.1.0.
&Z_support;
&curve_support;

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: This example buffers a point to approximate a circle, converts it to a curve, translates it by 1,2, and scales it by 3,4.
